answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is the force that pulls objects toward Earth or another planet. It makes things fall down instead of floating.
answers.everydayExample: When I drop my water bottle, it falls to the floor. That is gravity pulling it down.
answers.fallingObjects: I think the heavier book will hit first because it is heavier, but the air might slow the notebook.
answers.directionOfGravity: Gravity pulls down toward the ground because things always fall toward the floor.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is how much matter something has, weight is how heavy it feels because of gravity.
answers.spaceQuestion: On the Moon there is less gravity, so you can jump higher and fall slower.
answers.confusion: Not sure how gravity works in space with no air.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is a pull between objects with mass that brings them together.
answers.everydayExample: A basketball comes back down after you shoot it.
answers.fallingObjects: They should hit at the same time if air is not a factor, but in air the feather falls slower.
answers.directionOfGravity: Toward the center of Earth. You know because everything falls down no matter where you stand.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass stays the same, weight changes if gravity changes.
answers.spaceQuestion: In space you feel weightless because there is little gravity or you are in free fall.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is like a magnet that pulls things down to Earth.
answers.everydayExample: If I jump, I always come back down.
answers.fallingObjects: The heavy book falls faster because it has more gravity.
answers.directionOfGravity: Down toward the ground because Earth pulls on objects.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is the amount of stuff, weight is how heavy it is on Earth.
answers.spaceQuestion: On the Moon there is almost no gravity so things float.
answers.confusion: I do not get why heavier things fall faster or slower.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is a force between masses that causes attraction.
answers.everydayExample: Rain falls from clouds to the ground.
answers.fallingObjects: Both should fall together in a vacuum, but air resistance makes the feather slow.
answers.massVsWeight: Weight is the force of gravity on mass.
answers.spaceQuestion: In space you are still affected by gravity but you are in orbit so you float.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is what makes objects fall and keeps planets in orbit.
answers.everydayExample: My phone falls off the couch if it is near the edge.
answers.fallingObjects: The heavier book will fall first because it has more weight.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is constant, weight depends on gravity.
answers.spaceQuestion: On the Moon gravity is weaker so you weigh less.
answers.confusion: I wonder why gravity keeps the Moon around Earth.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is the force pulling objects toward each other.
answers.everydayExample: When I ride a bike downhill, I speed up because of gravity.
answers.fallingObjects: If air resistance is small, they hit together, but in air the lighter one is slower.
answers.directionOfGravity: Toward the center of the planet.
answers.massVsWeight: Weight is mass times gravity, mass is how much matter.
answers.spaceQuestion: There is still gravity in space, but astronauts are falling around Earth.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity pulls things down so they do not float away.
answers.everydayExample: A ball rolls back down a hill.
answers.fallingObjects: The heavy book falls faster because it is heavier.
answers.massVsWeight: I think mass and weight are basically the same.
answers.spaceQuestion: In space there is no gravity so everything floats.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is a force of attraction between masses like Earth and you.
answers.everydayExample: Throwing a ball up and it comes down.
answers.fallingObjects: They should land at the same time if you ignore air.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is how much matter; weight depends on gravity.
answers.spaceQuestion: On the Moon, gravity is weaker so you can jump higher.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is a pulling force that makes objects fall.
answers.everydayExample: Rain falls from the sky to the ground.
answers.fallingObjects: Heavier objects fall faster because they have more gravity.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is how big something is, weight is how heavy it is on Earth.
answers.spaceQuestion: In space there is no gravity so you float.
answers.confusion: I do not understand how gravity works in space.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is a force that attracts objects to each other.
answers.everydayExample: When I drop my keys they fall to the ground.
answers.fallingObjects: In a vacuum they land at the same time, in air the feather is slower.
answers.massVsWeight: Weight is the force of gravity on mass.
answers.spaceQuestion: On the Moon gravity is weaker so things are lighter.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity keeps us on the ground and makes things fall.
answers.everydayExample: A swing moves back down after being pushed.
answers.fallingObjects: The heavier book falls faster because it is heavier.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is how much stuff, weight is heaviness.
answers.spaceQuestion: In space there is no gravity so people float.
answers.confusion: Not sure about gravity if there is no air.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is the pull from Earth that causes acceleration downward.
answers.everydayExample: A pencil falls off the desk.
answers.fallingObjects: They fall together unless air resistance changes it.
answers.massVsWeight: Weight is mass times gravity.
answers.spaceQuestion: There is still gravity in space, but orbit feels like free fall.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is a force that pulls everything down.
answers.everydayExample: Snow falls from the sky.
answers.fallingObjects: The book hits first because it is heavier.
answers.massVsWeight: They are the same thing.
answers.spaceQuestion: No gravity in space so you float.
answers.confusion: I do not get what gravity is made of.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is an attractive force between objects.
answers.everydayExample: Jumping on a trampoline and coming back down.
answers.fallingObjects: They should land together if air is not slowing one.
answers.massVsWeight: Weight depends on gravity; mass does not.
answers.spaceQuestion: On the Moon gravity is less, so you weigh less.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is the force that pulls objects down to Earth.
answers.everydayExample: A soccer ball falls after you kick it up.
answers.fallingObjects: Heavy objects fall faster because they have more weight.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is amount of matter, weight is how heavy it feels.
answers.spaceQuestion: In space there is no gravity and everything floats.
answers.confusion: Not sure why air changes falling.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is a force that attracts objects to each other.
answers.everydayExample: A roller coaster goes down because of gravity.
answers.fallingObjects: They should hit together in a vacuum, but air slows the feather.
answers.massVsWeight: Weight is the force of gravity on mass.
answers.spaceQuestion: There is still gravity in space but you feel weightless in orbit.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity keeps us grounded and pulls objects down.
answers.everydayExample: Dropping a pen and it falls.
answers.fallingObjects: The heavier book falls faster because of more gravity.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is how much stuff, weight is how heavy it is on Earth.
answers.spaceQuestion: There is no gravity in space so people float.
answers.confusion: Not sure how gravity works without air.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is a force that pulls objects toward a planet.
answers.everydayExample: If I toss a coin, it comes back down.
answers.fallingObjects: They land together in a vacuum; air makes the feather slower.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is amount of matter, weight changes with gravity.
answers.spaceQuestion: On the Moon you weigh less because gravity is weaker.

answers.gravityDefinition: Gravity is the force that makes objects fall toward Earth.
answers.everydayExample: Water falls out of a cup if you tip it over.
answers.fallingObjects: The book falls faster because it is heavier.
answers.massVsWeight: Mass is how much matter, weight is how heavy it is.

answers.confusion: Not sure what happens to gravity in space.

answers.everydayExample: A book falls off a table.
answers.fallingObjects: They should fall together if you remove air resistance.
